landing.heroTitle: Chinese classics, rewritten as stories you can actually follow.
landing.heroSubtitle: A story-first reading project for classic Chinese novels. Start with Romance of the Three Kingdoms, where Liu Bei, Guan Yu, and Zhang Fei enter a broken empire.
landing.promiseTitle: A lower doorway into a great story.
landing.promiseCopy: Classic Chinese novels are full of loyalty, ambition, jokes, grudges, and impossible choices. This edition keeps the drive of the story close to the surface, so a new reader can follow the people before studying the world around them.

promiseItems.title: Story first
promiseItems.copy: Every passage has a goal, a conflict, a turn, and a reason to keep going.

promiseItems.title: Characters you can hold onto
promiseItems.copy: Liu Bei, Guan Yu, and Zhang Fei stay at the center, so the politics never float away from human stakes.

promiseItems.title: Text and comics together
promiseItems.copy: Read the prose, open the comic view, and move through the chapter without losing your place.

landing.currentTitle: Read Romance of the Three Kingdoms from the first chapter.
landing.currentCopy: The live edition begins with the empire in disorder, a recruitment notice in Zhuo County, and three men who are about to bind their lives together.
landing.currentPassageSummary: The empire is breaking. Yellow Turban rebels are rising. A notice in Zhuo County is about to push Liu Bei toward his first choice.
landing.libraryTitle: Starting with Three Kingdoms, built for more Chinese classics.
landing.libraryCopy: The first live book is Romance of the Three Kingdoms. The long-term direction is a reading shelf for the major Chinese classics, made with the same simple rule: let the story carry the reader in.

libraryItems.title: Romance of the Three Kingdoms
libraryItems.copy: War, loyalty, ambition, and three sworn brothers trying to matter in a collapsing world.

libraryItems.title: Journey to the West
libraryItems.copy: A pilgrimage, a monkey who will not behave, and a road full of tests.

libraryItems.title: Water Margin
libraryItems.copy: Outlaws, injustice, brotherhood, and the dangerous pull of a rebel camp.

libraryItems.title: Dream of the Red Chamber
libraryItems.copy: Family, desire, decline, and a household where tenderness and ruin live together.

landing.processTitle: Built passage by passage, not sprayed out in one draft.
landing.processCopy: Each section moves through planning, Chinese drafting, review, approved Chinese text, English adaptation, and comic integration. The point is not to explain everything. The point is to make the story readable.

processSteps.title: Plan the scene
processSteps.copy: A chapter is split into passages with a clear reader promise.

processSteps.title: Write and review
processSteps.copy: The Chinese draft is checked for clarity, drive, character consistency, and translation readiness.

processSteps.title: Adapt for reading
processSteps.copy: Approved text becomes English prose and comic reading assets for the website.

about.heroTitle: A reading project for Chinese classics, starting with Romance of the Three Kingdoms.
about.heroCopy: Read Chinese Classics is built for general readers who want the story before the lecture. The aim is not to flatten these books into summaries. The aim is to make them readable enough that a new reader can actually stay with them.
about.whyCopy: Many readers meet Chinese classics through fragments, quotes, school explanations, or intimidating translations. This project tries a simpler doorway: keep the narrative moving, lower the cultural barrier, and let the reader form attachments to the characters first.
about.whySanguoCopy: Romance of the Three Kingdoms has strong momentum, memorable entrances, and a cast that can carry long-form reading. Liu Bei, Guan Yu, and Zhang Fei give the story an emotional center while the wider world keeps opening around them.
about.processTitle: The text moves through an editorial pipeline, not a single prompt.

processSteps.title: Plan the chapter and passage
processSteps.copy: Each chapter is broken into passages with a micro-goal, a conflict, a turn, and a reading hook.

processSteps.title: Draft and review the Chinese text
processSteps.copy: The Chinese draft is checked for clarity, story drive, character consistency, and readiness for adaptation.

processSteps.title: Adapt for English and comics
processSteps.copy: Only approved Chinese text moves forward into English prose, comic text, and the website reading flow.

standards: Story first, not culture lecture first
Strong character attachment and clear scene movement
Low cultural barrier unless a tiny note is necessary
Shorter, cleaner prose that keeps the page turning
about.aiCopy: AI is part of the production workflow, but not the whole workflow. Planning, drafting, review, revision, adaptation, and presentation are treated as separate steps. The intended standard is AI-assisted production with human-directed editorial judgment.
about.statusCopy: The project is still moving chapter by chapter and passage by passage. The first live book is Romance of the Three Kingdoms, and the larger shelf is meant to grow from there.
about.ctaTitle: The best way to judge the project is still to read it.
about.ctaCopy: Start with the first chapter of Romance of the Three Kingdoms and see whether the story pulls you forward.

comic.description: Read this passage in comic mode, switch to text anytime.

feedback.disclosure: Text and comics are AI-assisted and edited by humans.

locale.chapterUnavailableTitle: This chapter is not in English yet.
locale.chapterUnavailable: English reading currently covers only chapters that already have approved English passages. You can read it in Chinese for now.
